response.justification: Bogdan brings roughly 20 years of broad software engineering experience, an MIT CS degree, and genuine hands-on AI-agent and MCP tooling work in his current role, which demonstrates adaptability and technical depth. However, the Principal Engineer — AI Strategy role is a director-level position requiring recognized, deep AI expertise with demonstrated AI products or platforms at scale, plus a strategic customer-advisory dimension. His AI exposure is recent (2025) and infrastructure-level rather than representing years of recognized AI innovation or thought leadership. His background is that of an excellent hands-on engineer and educator, not a strategic AI principal who shapes product direction and advises enterprise customers on cloud and AI adoption.
strengths: ~20 years of continuous software engineering experience across web, mobile, embedded, and infrastructure domains, meeting the tenure bar
Active, current engagement with AI-agent infrastructure and MCP (Model Context Protocol) frameworks, showing genuine pivot toward agentic AI tooling
MIT CS degree combined with entrepreneurial track record (GISCollective) and community knowledge-sharing (workshops, conference talks) demonstrates initiative beyond pure execution
concerns: AI expertise is largely new (post-April 2025) and tooling-level — no prior track record of recognized AI products, platforms, or thought leadership that the role explicitly requires
No evidence of director-level strategic advisory work: no experience advising enterprise customers, shaping product roadmaps, or influencing organizational AI strategy at scale
Cloud footprint is shallow — tooling use of AWS CDK and Kubernetes rather than deep cloud platform strategy, architecture, or partnership experience the role centers on
